_comment: Shared, frozen spec that every oracle generator (rust_oracle, python/gen.py, node/gen.mjs) reads, so all three sign IDENTICAL (seed, message, convention) tuples. This is what makes cross-oracle agreement (L5) meaningful. max_message_bytes must match native/sr25519_nif/src/lib.rs and Sr25519.max_message_bytes/0 (asserted by an L3 test). A message's optional "only" field pins it to a single oracle/seed/convention cell (any subset of those keys).

messages.name: utf8
messages.note: UTF-8 multibyte text: 'héllo wörld ☃ 日本語 𝄞' (2-4 byte sequences incl. astral plane)

messages.name: raw_binary
messages.note: raw bytes including NUL and high bytes

messages.name: hex_looking
messages.note: ASCII that looks like hex (must be treated as literal bytes, never decoded)

messages.name: prewrapped
messages.note: already <Bytes>-wrapped: polkadot-js u8aWrapBytes signs this AS-IS (passthrough), so it is signed under substrate_raw and verify_wrapped_bytes must not re-wrap it

messages.name: eth_prefixed
messages.note: Ethereum signed-message prefix + 'hello sr25519': u8aWrapBytes passthrough case two
